identity.short_name: Totient-sigma congruence with Hensel/2-adic/order-coupled square filter

problem_spec.summary: Let $n=p^kqr$ with distinct odd primes $p<q<r$ and $k\ge 2$. Solve the congruence $\varphi(n)x\equiv p^{k-1}qr\,\sigma(n)\pmod n$ via gcd-reduction and CRT, then impose a square filter $x/(qr)=y^2$ together with three coupled constraints on $y$: a Hensel-lifted quadratic condition modulo $p^2$, a fixed 2-adic square class modulo $2^a$, and an exact multiplicative order condition modulo $q$. These constraints force a unique least positive admissible $x_0$, and the output is $x_0\bmod 999$.
givens: $p,q,r$ are distinct odd primes with $p<q<r$, and $k$ is an integer with $k\ge 2$.
Define $n=p^kqr$. Let $\varphi(n)$ be Euler's totient function and $\sigma(n)$ the sum of positive divisors of $n$.
Fix integers $a\ge 3$, $u$ such that $u$ is a quadratic residue modulo $2^a$.
Fix an integer $c$ such that $y^2\equiv c\pmod p$ has a solution $y_0\not\equiv 0\pmod p$ (so it Hensel-lifts uniquely to modulo $p^2$).
Consider the congruence $\varphi(n)x\equiv p^{k-1}qr\,\sigma(n)\pmod n$ together with filters $v_q(x)=v_r(x)=1$ and $x/(qr)=y^2$ for some integer $y>0$.
Coupled constraints on $y$: $y^2\equiv c\pmod{p^2}$, $y^2\equiv u\pmod{2^a}$, and $\operatorname{ord}_q(y)=d$.
Let $x_0$ be the least positive $x$ satisfying all conditions.
ask: Determine the unique integer $N\in\{0,1,\dots,998\}$ such that $N\equiv x_0\pmod{999}$.

parameter(s).name: $p$
parameter(s).purpose: prime-power component $p^k$ creating the gcd bottleneck

parameter(s).name: $k$
parameter(s).purpose: controls the size of the forced gcd-reduction

parameter(s).name: $q$
parameter(s).purpose: prime factor forced into all solutions and the modulus for the order constraint

parameter(s).name: $r$
parameter(s).purpose: second forced prime factor and valuation constraint at $r$

parameter(s).name: $c$
parameter(s).purpose: target for the Hensel-lifted square condition $y^2\equiv c\pmod{p^2}$

parameter(s).name: $u$
parameter(s).domain: odd integer that is a square modulo $2^a$

invariant(s).name: modified_rhs_solvability
invariant(s).statement: With right-hand side $p^{k-1}qr\,\sigma(n)$, the divisibility condition $\gcd(\varphi(n),n)\mid p^{k-1}qr\,\sigma(n)$ is automatic.

invariant(s).name: crt_forces_qr_divisibility
invariant(s).statement: After gcd-reduction to modulus $n/p^{k-1}=pqr$, the reduced congruence forces $x\equiv 0\pmod q$ and $x\equiv 0\pmod r$, hence $qr\mid x$.

invariant(s).name: hensel_lift_square_constraint
invariant(s).statement: If $y^2\equiv c\pmod p$ has a solution $y_0\not\equiv 0\pmod p$, then $y^2\equiv c\pmod{p^2}$ has a unique lift in each of the two classes $y\equiv \pm y_0\pmod p$.

invariant(s).name: two_adic_square_class_constraint
invariant(s).statement: For $a\ge 3$, the constraint $y^2\equiv u\pmod{2^a}$ restricts $y$ to specific residue classes modulo $2^a$.

invariant(s).name: order_filter_constraint
invariant(s).statement: The constraint $\operatorname{ord}_q(y)=d$ restricts $y\bmod q$ to units of exact order $d$ in $(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times$.

invariant(s).name: coupled_crt_on_y
invariant(s).statement: The constraints on $y$ modulo $p^2$, $2^a$, and $q$ combine (CRT + order filtering) to a finite set of residue classes for $y$ modulo $p^2\cdot 2^a\cdot q$, from which the least positive admissible $y$ is determined.

essential_assumption(s): Guards $p\nmid(q-1)(r-1)$ and $q\nmid(r-1)$ so the reduced coefficient is invertible modulo $pqr$.
$c$ admits a simple-root Hensel lift from modulo $p$ to modulo $p^2$.
$u$ is a quadratic residue modulo $2^a$ so the 2-adic constraint is consistent.
The coupled constraints admit at least one solution, and the task selects the least positive one.
uniqueness_certificate(s):
Let $d_0=\gcd(\varphi(n),n)=p^{k-1}$. The congruence reduces to modulus $n/d_0=pqr$, and under the guards the reduced coefficient is coprime to $pqr$, hence the reduced congruence has a unique residue class; in particular it forces $qr\mid x$.
Writing $x=qr\,y^2$ (square filter) and imposing the constraints $y^2\equiv c\pmod{p^2}$ (Hensel-lifted), $y^2\equiv u\pmod{2^a}$ (2-adic square class), and $\operatorname{ord}_q(y)=d$ (order filter) restrict $y$ to finitely many residue classes modulo $p^2\cdot 2^a\cdot q$.
The least positive representative among these classes is unique, hence yields a unique least admissible $y$ and therefore a unique least admissible $x_0=qr\,y^2$. The normalized answer $N=x_0\bmod 999$ in $\{0,\dots,998\}$ is then unique.

technique(s).name: prime_power_closed_form_evaluation
technique(s).purpose: evaluate arithmetic functions for prime powers

technique(s).name: p_adic_exponent_tracking
technique(s).purpose: compute $d_0=\gcd(\varphi(n),n)$ and identify the forced reduction

technique(s).name: linear_congruence_gcd_reduction_and_inverse
technique(s).purpose: reduce to modulus $n/d_0$ and solve uniquely when the reduced coefficient is invertible
outputs: unique reduced class and full solution set

technique(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
inputs: reductions modulo the prime factors of $n/d_0$
technique(s).purpose: force $q\mid x$ and $r\mid x$ and obtain the full solution progression

technique(s).name: square_filter
technique(s).purpose: impose $x/(qr)$ is a perfect square by writing $x=qr\,y^2$

technique(s).name: hensel_lifting_quadratic_congruence
technique(s).purpose: lift a square root from modulo $p$ to modulo $p^2$

technique(s).name: multiplicative_order_characterization
technique(s).purpose: filter residues modulo $q$ by exact order

technique(s).name: coupled_CRT_on_parameters
technique(s).purpose: couple the invariants and determine the least admissible $y$

theorem(s).name: fundamental_theorem_of_arithmetic
theorem(s).role: prime factorization and coprimality underpin multiplicativity

theorem(s).name: linear_congruence_theorem
theorem(s).role: solvability criterion and solution structure for $ax\equiv b\pmod n$

theorem(s).name: Hensel_lemma_simple_root
theorem(s).role: unique lifting of roots from modulo $p$ to modulo $p^2$ when derivative is invertible

theorem(s).name: classification_of_squares_mod_2_power
theorem(s).role: odd squares modulo $2^a$ lie in fixed square classes for $a\ge 3$

critical_trick(s): Use $\gcd(\varphi(n),n)=p^{k-1}$ to force gcd-reduction and a unique reduced class modulo $pqr$, then write $x=qr\,y^2$ and couple Hensel lifting (mod $p^2$), a 2-adic square class (mod $2^a$), and an exact order condition (mod $q$) to pin down the unique least admissible solution.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: gcd, integer square root, modular arithmetic, valuation loops

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: totient, divisor_sigma, modular inverses, multiplicative order for verification

task(s).name: direct_congruence_check
task(s).reason: verify the congruence and all modular/valuation constraints for the derived $x_0$

task(s).name: minimality_regression
task(s).reason: confirm no smaller positive $y$ satisfies the coupled constraints (bounded scan up to $y_0$)
